refined_fr.refined_fr: Capture and represent worksite spatial features in a machine-readable model.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR bundles three distinct obligations: (1) terrain analysis, (2) obstacle identification, and (3) work-zone representation. Each is a separate functional concern with different data sources, processing logic, and stakeholder consumption patterns. The refined FR is solution-neutral (avoids 'mapping/modeling' terminology which implies a specific DP) and focuses on the core problem: making spatial features available in a structured, queryable form. Splitting into atomic sub-FRs allows independent verification and design of each capability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Confirm that a worksite model exists in a defined schema/format, contains spatial coordinates and feature classifications, and is accessible to downstream planning and execution functions per acceptance criteria.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-006.1: Acquire terrain elevation and surface properties across the worksite.
FR-NGHE-006.2: Identify and locate static obstacles (structures, vegetation, utilities) within the worksite boundary.
FR-NGHE-006.3: Define and represent work-zone boundaries and operational areas.
FR-NGHE-006.4: Integrate captured spatial features into a unified worksite model accessible to planning and execution functions.
refined_fr.note: The parent FR (CR-NGHE-006) remains a coordination node; each sub-FR is atomic and can be independently designed, verified, and assigned to implementation modules.

candidate_dps: DP1: Acquire spatial feature data through multi-modal sensing (vision, range, inertial) and fuse into a unified coordinate frame; store as a structured spatial graph with feature attributes (geometry, classification, safety constraints); expose via query interface for downstream planning/execution consumers.

DP2: Decompose worksite into discrete zones; assign zone-level feature inventories (obstacles, hazards, access points) via manual annotation or semi-automated detection; maintain zone-feature mappings in a relational schema; provide zone-based query and update APIs.

DP3: Capture worksite features as a voxel-grid occupancy model with semantic labels; support incremental updates from streaming sensor data; provide spatial query (collision, proximity, traversability) and export to planning-compatible formats.

DP4: Integrate external worksite blueprints/CAD models as reference geometry; overlay real-time sensor observations to detect deviations and dynamic features; maintain a hybrid static-dynamic feature registry; reconcile conflicts via confidence scoring.

DP5: Establish a feature capture pipeline: raw sensor ingestion → feature detection (rule-based or learned classifiers) → spatial registration → conflict resolution → persistent model store; expose model via standardized query language; support versioning and audit trails for safety compliance.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP5: Establish a feature capture pipeline: raw sensor ingestion → feature detection (rule-based or learned classifiers) → spatial registration → conflict resolution → persistent model store; expose model via standardized query language; support versioning and audit trails for safety compliance.

potential_sources: Feature detection logic tightly coupled to sensor modality (risk: changing sensor type requires retraining classifiers). Mitigation: abstract sensor input as normalized feature candidates; keep detection logic modular.

Spatial registration assumes consistent coordinate frame initialization (risk: poor calibration propagates through entire model). Mitigation: explicit calibration validation step; support re-registration on demand.

Conflict resolution heuristics may be domain-specific (e.g., safety-critical features override dynamic observations). Mitigation: parameterize conflict rules; expose as configurable policy tied to safety plan.

Query interface design must remain agnostic to downstream planner/executor assumptions. Risk: over-specifying query semantics locks in one planning paradigm. Mitigation: provide low-level spatial primitives (point, polygon, volume queries) and let consumers compose higher-level logic.
coupling_check.notes: DP5 is preferred because it explicitly stages the problem (ingest → detect → register → resolve → store → query), making each stage independently testable and replaceable. The pipeline structure supports incremental refinement and accommodates both static (blueprint-derived) and dynamic (sensor-observed) features. Versioning and audit trails directly support the safety plan and operational policy constraints.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Worksite features are sufficiently stable during a capture cycle (minutes to hours). Risk: highly dynamic environments (e.g., moving equipment, personnel) may cause stale model data. Mitigation: define model refresh frequency and staleness thresholds; flag dynamic features separately.

Assumption: Feature detection classifiers (if learned) generalize across similar worksites. Risk: domain shift (new site type, lighting, occlusion patterns) degrades detection accuracy. Mitigation: establish ground-truth labeling protocol; monitor detection confidence; support active learning feedback loops.

Assumption: Spatial registration errors are bounded and correctable. Risk: systematic calibration drift or loop-closure failures in large sites. Mitigation: implement drift detection; support manual correction workflows; log registration confidence per region.

Assumption: Safety plan and operational policies are stable during model lifecycle. Risk: policies change mid-operation (e.g., new hazard zone declared). Mitigation: version policies alongside model; support policy-driven feature re-classification.

Assumption: Downstream planning/execution functions can tolerate model uncertainty and incompleteness. Risk: missing or misclassified features cause safety violations. Mitigation: define minimum completeness/accuracy SLOs; implement validation gates before model release.

Assumption: Coordinate frame is globally consistent across all sensor observations. Risk: multi-agent or multi-site deployments may have local frames. Mitigation: support frame transformations; document frame definitions in model metadata.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP5 to explicitly separate concerns: (1) **Sensor Abstraction Layer**: normalize heterogeneous sensor streams into feature candidate streams (decouples detection from hardware). (2) **Feature Detection Module**: apply classifiers (rule-based or learned) to candidates; output labeled features with confidence scores. (3) **Spatial Registration Module**: align features into a shared coordinate frame; track registration uncertainty. (4) **Conflict Resolution Policy Engine**: apply safety-plan-driven rules to reconcile overlapping or contradictory features; log decisions for audit. (5) **Model Store & Query Service**: persist versioned feature graph; expose query API (spatial, semantic, temporal filters). This decomposition enables independent testing, policy updates without model rebuild, and clear ownership boundaries for implementation modules. Additionally, introduce an explicit **Model Validation & Certification** stage before marking a model as 'available to planning/execution,' ensuring acceptance criteria are met and safety constraints are satisfied.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Centralized feature capture pipeline including sensor ingestion, feature detection, spatial registration, conflict resolution, persistent model storage, standardized query interface, versioning, and audit trail management for safety-critical operations.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Raw sensor data ingestion from Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems; feeds feature detection pipeline

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Emits safety-relevant feature detections, conflict resolutions, and audit events to downstream safety consumers

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives query requests for model state; returns versioned feature data and historical audit trails

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Optional: distributed model synchronization and remote audit log replication for multi-vehicle or cloud-based compliance archival

module_containment_scores.notes: Excellent fit: feature capture, conflict resolution, versioning, and audit trails are inherently safety-critical concerns. Persistent model store and query language naturally belong in a centralized safety domain. Minor diffusion: raw telemetry ingestion may require lightweight adapters in Vehicle/Teleoperation, but core pipeline logic remains contained.

Moderate fit if NGHE is a top-level orchestrator. However, feature capture is a specialized safety function; placing it at solution level risks diluting safety ownership and complicating audit accountability. Diffusion: feature detection logic, versioning, and audit trails would leak into Vehicle and Teleoperation for local caching or redundancy.

Poor fit: feature capture pipeline is not vehicle-specific; it is a system-wide safety service. Placing it in Vehicle creates tight coupling to vehicle state and forces replication across multiple vehicle instances. Diffusion: conflict resolution and audit trails would need to propagate to Teleoperation and Safety Services, violating single responsibility.

Poor fit: teleoperation is a control/command interface, not a data aggregation or safety model store. Feature capture is independent of teleoperation mode. Diffusion: model versioning and audit trails would require bidirectional coupling to Vehicle and Safety Services, creating circular dependencies.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Safety Services is the natural owner of the feature capture pipeline because: (1) feature detection and conflict resolution are safety-critical functions requiring centralized governance; (2) versioning and audit trails are compliance requirements inherent to safety domains; (3) a standardized query language for model state is a safety interface responsibility; (4) persistent model store ensures deterministic, auditable state for safety reasoning. Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces provide clean input/output boundaries; Tasking enables safe query access; Network Link supports distributed compliance archival.
containment_risks: Risk: Raw sensor ingestion may require Vehicle-specific adapters (e.g., CAN bus parsing, IMU calibration) that could diffuse into Vehicle module. Mitigation: define a thin, standardized Telemetry contract that Vehicle adapters implement; Safety Services consumes only normalized Telemetry.

Risk: Feature detection classifiers (learned or rule-based) may be tuned per vehicle type, tempting local caching in Vehicle. Mitigation: Safety Services maintains a versioned model registry; Vehicle queries it via Tasking interface rather than caching locally.

Risk: Conflict resolution logic may require domain-specific heuristics (e.g., sensor fusion weights) that could leak into Teleoperation or Vehicle. Mitigation: encapsulate all conflict resolution rules within Safety Services; expose only resolved feature state via SafetyAlert.

Risk: Audit trail volume may grow large; distributed replication via Network Link could introduce consistency issues. Mitigation: define clear audit log schema and versioning strategy; use Network Link only for asynchronous, eventually-consistent archival.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High. Acceptance criteria require that a worksite model is generated and available to planning/execution functions for an agreed set of site features. Safety Services module provides persistent model store with standardized query interface (Tasking) and versioned audit trails, enabling direct verification that model state is accessible, auditable, and compliant with safety policies. Telemetry ingestion and SafetyAlert emission are observable and testable.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear. FR maps to DP5 (feature capture pipeline with versioning and audit) and is allocated to Safety Services IM boundary. Ownership is unambiguous: Safety Services owns the complete pipeline from sensor ingestion through persistent storage and query exposure. External interfaces (Telemetry in, SafetyAlert out, Tasking query, Network Link sync) define clean responsibility boundaries.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP5 fully addresses FR. The feature capture pipeline (raw sensor ingestion → feature detection → spatial registration → conflict resolution → persistent model store) directly solves the requirement to capture and represent worksite spatial features in machine-readable form. Standardized query language and versioning/audit trails satisfy safety compliance and operational policy requirements. DP is sufficiently detailed to guide IM allocation.

diffusion_risks: Risk: Raw sensor ingestion adapters (CAN bus parsing, IMU calibration) may leak into Vehicle module if not strictly bounded by Telemetry contract. Mitigation: enforce thin adapter pattern; Vehicle implements only normalized Telemetry interface; Safety Services consumes only standardized telemetry schema.

Risk: Feature detection classifiers (rule-based or learned) may be duplicated or cached locally in Vehicle if versioning strategy is unclear. Mitigation: Safety Services maintains single source of truth for all classifier versions; Vehicle queries via Tasking interface; no local caching of detection logic.

Risk: Conflict resolution heuristics (sensor fusion weights, priority rules) may diffuse into Teleoperation or Vehicle if domain-specific tuning is required per vehicle type. Mitigation: encapsulate all conflict resolution within Safety Services; expose only resolved feature state via SafetyAlert; parameterize heuristics within Safety Services, not in external modules.

Risk: Audit trail volume and distributed replication via Network Link could introduce consistency or ordering issues if schema and versioning are not rigorously defined. Mitigation: define explicit audit log schema with monotonic versioning; use Network Link for asynchronous, eventually-consistent archival only; do not rely on Network Link for real-time safety decisions.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Excellent containment (0.92 score). Safety Services is the natural owner: feature capture, conflict resolution, versioning, and audit trails are inherently safety-critical and require centralized governance for compliance. Persistent model store and standardized query language belong in a centralized safety domain. Minor diffusion risk is limited to lightweight telemetry adapters in Vehicle/Teleoperation, which can be mitigated by strict interface contracts. Alternative allocations (NGHE, Vehicle, Teleoperation) all score poorly (0.35–0.65) due to lack of safety ownership, tight coupling, or circular dependencies. Safety Services allocation is strongly recommended.

recommended_revisions: Formalize Telemetry contract: define schema for normalized sensor data (e.g., JSON or protobuf) that Vehicle and Teleoperation adapters must implement. Safety Services consumes only this contract; no Vehicle-specific parsing logic in Safety Services.

Establish feature detection versioning strategy: Safety Services maintains a versioned registry of all classifiers (rule-based and learned). Document how Vehicle queries and caches classifier versions; prohibit local caching of detection logic.

Encapsulate conflict resolution rules: all sensor fusion weights, priority heuristics, and resolution logic remain within Safety Services. Expose only resolved feature state via SafetyAlert; parameterize heuristics as Safety Services configuration, not external inputs.

Define audit log schema and monotonic versioning: specify audit trail structure (timestamp, event type, actor, feature delta, resolution outcome) and versioning scheme. Ensure all audit events are immutable and ordered.

Clarify Network Link semantics: document whether Network Link is used for real-time model synchronization or asynchronous archival only. If asynchronous, define eventual consistency guarantees and conflict resolution for distributed audit logs.

Specify Tasking query language: define the standardized query interface (e.g., REST API, GraphQL, or domain-specific language) that planning/execution functions use to retrieve versioned feature data and audit trails. Include query examples for common safety-critical scenarios (e.g., 'retrieve all features detected in zone X since timestamp T with audit trail').

Document safety policy integration: map worksite safety plan and operational policies to specific feature types, conflict resolution rules, and audit trail requirements. Ensure Safety Services configuration reflects these policies.

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass with high confidence. Verification feasibility is high, allocation clarity is clear, and containment score is excellent (0.92). However, the recommended_revisions identify critical formalization gaps that must be resolved at the next decomposition level to prevent diffusion risks and ensure safety compliance. The five diffusion risks (telemetry adapter leakage, classifier duplication, conflict resolution heuristics diffusion, audit trail consistency, and Network Link semantics) are well-characterized but require sub-FR decomposition to establish concrete interface contracts, versioning strategies, and audit schemas. Proceeding to next-level sub-FRs will operationalize these mitigations and make them verifiable. Safety criticality is high, justifying rigorous decomposition before implementation.
next_level_sub_frs: FR5.1: Normalize and standardize telemetry schema from heterogeneous vehicle sensors (CAN bus, IMU, GNSS, LiDAR) into a unified, versioned contract that Vehicle and Teleoperation adapters must implement without domain-specific parsing logic in Safety Services.

FR5.2: Maintain a centralized, versioned registry of all feature detection classifiers (rule-based and learned) with explicit version control, query interface, and prohibition on local caching in Vehicle or Teleoperation modules.

FR5.3: Encapsulate and parameterize all sensor fusion conflict resolution rules (weights, priority heuristics, resolution logic) within Safety Services configuration, exposing only resolved feature state via SafetyAlert without external tuning inputs.

FR5.4: Define and enforce an immutable, monotonically-versioned audit log schema (timestamp, event type, actor, feature delta, resolution outcome) with strict ordering guarantees for all feature capture, detection, and conflict resolution events.

FR5.5: Clarify and formalize Network Link semantics for distributed audit trail replication, specifying whether synchronization is real-time or asynchronous-only, and defining eventual consistency guarantees and conflict resolution for distributed audit logs.

FR5.6: Specify a standardized, queryable interface (e.g., REST API, GraphQL, or domain-specific language) for planning/execution functions to retrieve versioned feature data, audit trails, and resolution provenance with concrete examples for safety-critical scenarios.

revise.why: DP5 is logically sound but insufficiently detailed to prevent the five identified diffusion risks. Revise DP5 to explicitly include: (1) telemetry normalization layer with strict schema contract, (2) centralized classifier versioning and registry, (3) parameterized conflict resolution engine with no external inputs, (4) immutable audit log with monotonic versioning, (5) Network Link semantics (real-time vs. asynchronous), and (6) standardized query interface specification. These revisions operationalize the recommended_revisions and make DP5 sufficiently concrete to guide IM allocation and prevent architectural diffusion.

assumptions: Vehicle and Teleoperation modules can be constrained to implement only the normalized Telemetry contract without requiring Safety Services to parse vehicle-specific sensor formats.
Feature detection classifiers (rule-based and learned) can be versioned and centrally managed without requiring per-vehicle tuning or local caching.
Conflict resolution heuristics can be fully parameterized within Safety Services configuration without requiring external inputs from Vehicle, Teleoperation, or domain-specific modules.
Audit trail volume and Network Link replication can be managed with eventual consistency semantics (i.e., real-time safety decisions do not depend on distributed audit log synchronization).
Planning/execution functions can be satisfied with a standardized query interface (REST, GraphQL, or DSL) without requiring custom query logic per use case.
Safety policy integration can be achieved through explicit mapping of worksite safety plans to feature types, conflict resolution rules, and audit trail requirements in Safety Services configuration.
open_risks: Telemetry adapter leakage: Vehicle or Teleoperation modules may implement domain-specific sensor parsing logic if Telemetry contract is not rigorously enforced. Mitigation: formalize Telemetry schema (FR5.1) and establish code review gates to prevent adapter logic outside Safety Services.

Classifier duplication: Feature detection logic may be cached or duplicated locally in Vehicle if versioning strategy is unclear. Mitigation: establish centralized classifier registry (FR5.2) with explicit prohibition on local caching and audit logging of all classifier queries.

Conflict resolution diffusion: Sensor fusion heuristics may leak into Vehicle or Teleoperation if per-vehicle tuning is required. Mitigation: parameterize all heuristics within Safety Services (FR5.3) and document how vehicle-specific tuning is achieved through configuration, not code.

Audit trail consistency: Distributed replication via Network Link could introduce ordering or consistency issues if schema is not rigorously defined. Mitigation: define immutable, monotonically-versioned audit log (FR5.4) and clarify Network Link semantics (FR5.5) to ensure eventual consistency only.

Query interface complexity: Planning/execution functions may require custom query logic if standardized interface is too restrictive. Mitigation: specify concrete query examples (FR5.6) and validate against representative safety-critical scenarios before implementation.
evidence_needed: Formalized Telemetry schema (JSON/protobuf) with examples of normalized sensor data from CAN bus, IMU, GNSS, and LiDAR sources.
Classifier versioning strategy document: how versions are assigned, stored, queried, and cached (or not cached) in Vehicle/Teleoperation.
Conflict resolution parameterization specification: list of all heuristics, weights, and priority rules; how they are configured in Safety Services; examples of per-vehicle tuning via configuration.
Audit log schema specification: structure (timestamp, event type, actor, feature delta, resolution outcome), versioning scheme, immutability guarantees, and ordering semantics.
Network Link semantics document: clarification of real-time vs. asynchronous replication, eventual consistency guarantees, and conflict resolution strategy for distributed audit logs.

Standardized query interface specification: API definition (REST, GraphQL, or DSL) with concrete examples for safety-critical scenarios (e.g., 'retrieve all features in zone X since timestamp T with audit trail').
Safety policy integration mapping: document linking worksite safety plans to feature types, conflict resolution rules, and audit trail requirements in Safety Services configuration.
Verification plan for each sub-FR: acceptance criteria, test cases, and traceability to safety compliance requirements.
